/**
 * Callbacks invoked by the FilterManager to pass filter data/events back to the caller.
 */
class FilterManagerCallbacks {
public:
  virtual ~FilterManagerCallbacks() = default;

  /**
   * Called when the provided headers have been encoded by all the filters in the chain.
   * @param response_headers the encoded headers.
   * @param end_stream whether this is a header only response.
   */
  virtual void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, bool end_stream) PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the provided 100 Continue headers have been encoded by all the filters in the
   * chain.
   * @param response_headers the encoded headers.
   */
  virtual void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the provided data has been encoded by all filters in the chain.
   * @param data the encoded data.
   * @param end_stream whether this is the end of the response.
   */
  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the provided trailers have been encoded by all filters in the chain.
   * @param trailers the encoded trailers.
   */
  virtual void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the provided metadata has been encoded by all filters in the chain.
   * @param trailers the encoded trailers.
   */
  virtual void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata) PURE;

  /**
   * Injects request trailers into a stream that originally did not have request trailers.
   */
  virtual void setRequestTrailers(RequestTrailerMapPtr&& request_trailers) PURE;

  /**
   * Passes ownership of received informational headers to the parent. This may be called multiple
   * times in the case of multiple upstream calls.
   */
  virtual void setInformationalHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) PURE;

  /**
   * Passes ownership of received response headers to the parent. This may be called multiple times
   * in the case of multiple upstream calls.
   */
  virtual void setResponseHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) PURE;

  /**
   * Passes ownership of received response trailers to the parent. This may be called multiple times
   * in the case of multiple upstream calls.
   */
  virtual void setResponseTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& response_trailers) PURE;

  /**
   * Optionally updates response code stats based on the details in the headers.
   */
  virtual void chargeStats(const ResponseHeaderMap& /*headers*/) {}

  // TODO(snowp): We should consider moving filter access to headers/trailers to happen via the
  // callbacks instead of via the encode/decode callbacks on the filters.

  /**
   * The downstream request headers if set.
   */
  virtual RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() PURE;

  /**
   * The downstream request trailers if present.
   */
  virtual RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() PURE;

  /**
   * Retrieves a pointer to the continue headers set via the call to setInformationalHeaders.
   */
  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() PURE;

  /**
   * Retrieves a pointer to the response headers set via the last call to setResponseHeaders.
   * Note that response headers might be set multiple times (e.g. if a local reply is issued after
   * headers have been received but before headers have been encoded), so it is not safe in general
   * to assume that any set of headers will be valid for the duration of a stream.
   */
  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() PURE;

  /**
   * Retrieves a pointer to the last response trailers set via setResponseTrailers.
   * Note that response trailers might be set multiple times, so it is not safe in general to assume
   * that any set of trailers will be valid for the duration of the stream.
   */
  virtual ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() PURE;

  /**
   * Called after encoding has completed.
   */
  virtual void endStream() PURE;

  /**
   * Attempt to send GOAWAY and close the connection.
   */
  virtual void sendGoAwayAndClose(bool graceful = false) PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the stream write buffer is no longer above the low watermark.
   */
  virtual void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the stream write buffer is above above the high watermark.
   */
  virtual void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() PURE;

  /**
   * Called when request activity indicates that the request timeout should be disarmed.
   */
  virtual void disarmRequestTimeout() PURE;

  /**
   * Called when stream activity indicates that the stream idle timeout should be reset.
   */
  virtual void resetIdleTimer() PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the stream should be re-created, e.g. for an internal redirect.
   */
  virtual void recreateStream(StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr filter_state) PURE;

  /**
   * Called when the stream should be reset.
   */
  virtual void
  resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason = Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset,
              absl::string_view transport_failure_reason = "") PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the upgrade map for the current route entry.
   */
  virtual const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgradeMap() PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the cluster info for the current route entry.
   */
  virtual Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the current active span.
   */
  virtual Tracing::Span& activeSpan() PURE;

  // TODO(snowp): It might make more sense to pass (optional?) counters to the FM instead of
  // calling back out to the AS to record them.
  /**
   * Called when a stream fails due to the response data being too large.
   */
  virtual void onResponseDataTooLarge() PURE;

  /**
   * Called when a stream fails due to the request data being too large.
   */
  virtual void onRequestDataTooLarge() PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the Http1StreamEncoderOptions associated with the response encoder.
   */
  virtual Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks for upstream HTTP filters.
   */
  virtual OptRef<UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> upstreamCallbacks() { return {}; }

  /**
   * Called when a local reply is made by the filter manager.
   * @param code the response code of the local reply.
   */
  virtual void onLocalReply(Code code) PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the tracing configuration to use for this stream.
   */
  virtual OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the tracked scope to use for this stream.
   */
  virtual const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() PURE;

  /**
   * Returns the DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks for downstream HTTP filters.
   */
  virtual OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() { return {}; }
  /**
   * Returns if close from the upstream is to be handled with half-close semantics.
   * This is used for HTTP/1.1 codec.
   */
  virtual bool isHalfCloseEnabled() PURE;
};

/**
 * This class allows the remote address to be overridden for HTTP stream info. This is used for
 * XFF handling. This is required to avoid providing stream info with a non-const connection info
 * provider. Private inheritance from ConnectionInfoProvider is used to make sure users get the
 * address provider via the normal getter.
 */
class OverridableRemoteConnectionInfoSetterStreamInfo : public StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl,
                                                        private Network::ConnectionInfoProvider {
public:
  using StreamInfoImpl::StreamInfoImpl;

  void setDownstreamRemoteAddress(
      const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& downstream_remote_address) {
    // TODO(rgs1): we should assert overridden_downstream_remote_address_ is nullptr,
    // but we are currently relaxing this as a workaround to:
    //
    // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/pull/14432#issuecomment-758167614
    overridden_downstream_remote_address_ = downstream_remote_address;
  }

  // StreamInfo::StreamInfo
  const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& downstreamAddressProvider() const override {
    return *this;
  }

  // Network::ConnectionInfoProvider
  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& localAddress() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().localAddress();
  }
  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& directLocalAddress() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().directLocalAddress();
  }
  bool localAddressRestored() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().localAddressRestored();
  }
  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& remoteAddress() const override {
    return overridden_downstream_remote_address_ != nullptr
               ? overridden_downstream_remote_address_
               : StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().remoteAddress();
  }
  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& directRemoteAddress() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().directRemoteAddress();
  }
  absl::string_view requestedServerName() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().requestedServerName();
  }
  const std::vector<std::string>& requestedApplicationProtocols() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().requestedApplicationProtocols();
  }
  absl::optional<uint64_t> connectionID() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().connectionID();
  }
  absl::optional<absl::string_view> interfaceName() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().interfaceName();
  }
  Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr sslConnection() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().sslConnection();
  }
  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override {
    StreamInfoImpl::dumpState(os, indent_level);

    const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);
    os << spaces << "OverridableRemoteConnectionInfoSetterStreamInfo " << this
       << DUMP_MEMBER_AS(remoteAddress(), remoteAddress()->asStringView())
       << DUMP_MEMBER_AS(directRemoteAddress(), directRemoteAddress()->asStringView())
       << DUMP_MEMBER_AS(localAddress(), localAddress()->asStringView()) << "\n";
  }
  absl::string_view ja3Hash() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().ja3Hash();
  }
  absl::string_view ja4Hash() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().ja4Hash();
  }
  const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& roundTripTime() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().roundTripTime();
  }
  OptRef<const Network::FilterChainInfo> filterChainInfo() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().filterChainInfo();
  }
  OptRef<const Network::ListenerInfo> listenerInfo() const override {
    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().listenerInfo();
  }

private:
  Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr overridden_downstream_remote_address_;
};

/**
 * FilterManager manages decoding a request through a series of decoding filter and the encoding
 * of the resulting response.
 */
class FilterManager : public ScopeTrackedObject, Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http> {
public:
  FilterManager(FilterManagerCallbacks& filter_manager_callbacks, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,
                OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection, uint64_t stream_id,
                Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account, bool proxy_100_continue,
                uint64_t buffer_limit)
      : filter_manager_callbacks_(filter_manager_callbacks), dispatcher_(dispatcher),
        connection_(connection), stream_id_(stream_id), account_(std::move(account)),
        proxy_100_continue_(proxy_100_continue), buffer_limit_(buffer_limit) {}

  ~FilterManager() override {
    ASSERT(state_.destroyed_);
    ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ == 0);
  }

  // ScopeTrackedObject
  OptRef<const StreamInfo::StreamInfo> trackedStream() const override { return streamInfo(); }
  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const override {
    const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);
    os << spaces << "FilterManager " << this << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.has_1xx_headers_)
       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_)
       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_)
       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.observed_decode_end_stream_)
       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.observed_encode_end_stream_) << "\n";

    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders());
    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());
    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders());
    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers());
    DUMP_DETAILS(&streamInfo());
  }

  void log(const Formatter::Context log_context) {
    for (const auto& log_handler : access_log_handlers_) {
      log_handler->log(log_context, streamInfo());
    }
  }

  const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& accessLogHandlers() { return access_log_handlers_; }

  void onStreamComplete() {
    for (auto filter : filters_) {
      filter->onStreamComplete();
    }
  }

  void destroyFilters() {
    state_.destroyed_ = true;

    for (auto filter : filters_) {
      filter->onDestroy();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Decodes the provided headers starting at the first filter in the chain.
   * @param headers the headers to decode.
   * @param end_stream whether the request is header only.
   */
  void decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {
    state_.observed_decode_end_stream_ = end_stream;
    decodeHeaders(nullptr, headers, end_stream);
  }

  /**
   * Decodes the provided data starting at the first filter in the chain.
   * @param data the data to decode.
   * @param end_stream whether this data is the end of the request.
   */
  void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {
    state_.observed_decode_end_stream_ = end_stream;
    decodeData(nullptr, data, end_stream, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);
  }

  /**
   * Decodes the provided trailers starting at the first filter in the chain.
   * @param trailers the trailers to decode.
   */
  void decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers) {
    state_.observed_decode_end_stream_ = true;
    decodeTrailers(nullptr, trailers);
  }

  /**
   * Decodes the provided metadata starting at the first filter in the chain.
   * @param metadata_map the metadata to decode.
   */
  void decodeMetadata(MetadataMap& metadata_map) { decodeMetadata(nullptr, metadata_map); }

  void disarmRequestTimeout();

  /**
   * If end_stream is true, marks encoding as complete. This is a noop if end_stream is false.
   * @param end_stream whether encoding is complete.
   */
  void maybeEndEncode(bool end_stream);

  /**
   * If terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream is true, marks decoding as complete. This is a noop if
   * terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream is false.
   * @param end_stream whether decoding is complete.
   */
  void maybeEndDecode(bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);

  void checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();

  virtual void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,
                              const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,
                              const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,
                              absl::string_view details) PURE;

  void resetStream(StreamResetReason reason, absl::string_view transport_failure_reason);

  /**
   * Executes a prepared, but not yet propagated, local reply.
   * Prepared local replies can occur in the decoder filter chain iteration.
   */
  virtual void executeLocalReplyIfPrepared() PURE;

  // Possibly increases buffer_limit_ to the value of limit.
  void setBufferLimit(uint64_t limit);

  /**
   * @return uint64_t the current buffer limit.
   */
  uint64_t bufferLimit() const { return buffer_limit_; }

  /**
   * @return bool whether any above high watermark triggers are currently active
   */
  bool aboveHighWatermark() { return high_watermark_count_ != 0; }

  // Pass on watermark callbacks to watermark subscribers. This boils down to passing watermark
  // events for this stream and the downstream connection to the router filter.
  void callHighWatermarkCallbacks();
  void callLowWatermarkCallbacks();

  void requestHeadersInitialized() {
    if (Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Head ==
        filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()->getMethodValue()) {
      state_.is_head_request_ = true;
    }
    state_.is_grpc_request_ =
        Grpc::Common::isGrpcRequestHeaders(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders().ref());
  }

  /**
   * Marks local processing as complete.
   * TODO(yanvlasov): deprecate and decommission this function.
   */
  void setLocalComplete() {
    state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;
    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;
  }

  /**
   * Whether the filters have been destroyed.
   */
  bool destroyed() const { return state_.destroyed_; }

  /**
   * Whether remote processing has been marked as complete.
   */
  virtual bool decoderObservedEndStream() const { return state_.observed_decode_end_stream_; }

  /**
   * Instructs the FilterManager to not create a filter chain. This makes it possible to issue
   * a local reply without the overhead of creating and traversing the filters.
   */
  void skipFilterChainCreation() {
    ASSERT(!state_.create_chain_result_.created());
    state_.create_chain_result_ = CreateChainResult(true);
  }

  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() PURE;
  virtual const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() const PURE;

  enum class UpgradeResult : uint8_t { UpgradeUnneeded, UpgradeAccepted, UpgradeRejected };

  /**
   * Filter chain creation result.
   */
  class CreateChainResult {
  public:
    CreateChainResult() = default;

    /**
     * @param created whether the filter chain was created.
     * @param upgrade the upgrade result.
     */
    CreateChainResult(bool created, UpgradeResult upgrade = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded)
        : created_(created), upgrade_(upgrade) {}

    /**
     * @return whether the filter chain was created.
     */
    bool created() const { return created_; }
    /**
     * @return whether the upgrade was accepted.
     */
    bool upgradeAccepted() const { return upgrade_ == UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted; }
    /**
     * @return whether the upgrade was rejected.
     */
    bool upgradeRejected() const { return upgrade_ == UpgradeResult::UpgradeRejected; }

  private:
    bool created_ = false;
    UpgradeResult upgrade_ = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;
  };

  /**
   * Set up the Encoder/Decoder filter chain.
   * @param filter_chain_factory the factory to create the filter chain.
   */
  CreateChainResult createFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory);

  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const { return connection_; }

  uint64_t streamId() const { return stream_id_; }
  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account() const { return account_; }

  Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedRequestData() { return buffered_request_data_; }

  void contextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack);

  void onDownstreamReset() { state_.saw_downstream_reset_ = true; }
  bool sawDownstreamReset() { return state_.saw_downstream_reset_; }

  virtual bool shouldLoadShed() { return false; };

  void sendGoAwayAndClose(bool graceful = false) {
    // Stop filter chain iteration by checking encoder or decoder chain.
    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {
      state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;
    } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {
      state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;
    }
    filter_manager_callbacks_.sendGoAwayAndClose(graceful);
  }

protected:
  struct State {
    State() = default;
    uint32_t filter_call_state_{0};

    // Set after decoder filter chain has completed iteration. Prevents further calls to decoder
    // filters. This flag is used to determine stream completion when the independent half-close is
    // enabled.
    bool decoder_filter_chain_complete_{};

    // Set after encoder filter chain has completed iteration. Prevents further calls to encoder
    // filters. This flag is used to determine stream completion when the independent half-close is
    // enabled.
    bool encoder_filter_chain_complete_{};

    // Set `true` when the filter manager observes end stream on the decoder path (from downstream
    // client) before iteration of the decoder filter chain begins. This flag is used for setting
    // end_stream value when resuming decoder filter chain iteration.
    bool observed_decode_end_stream_{};
    // Set `true` when the filter manager observes end stream on the encoder path (from upstream
    // server or Envoy's local reply) before iteration of the encoder filter chain begins. This flag
    // is used for setting end_stream value when resuming encoder filter chain iteration.
    bool observed_encode_end_stream_{};

    // By default, we will assume there are no 1xx. If encode1xxHeaders
    // is ever called, this is set to true so commonContinue resumes processing the 1xx.
    bool has_1xx_headers_{};
    // These two are latched on initial header read, to determine if the original headers
    // constituted a HEAD or gRPC request, respectively.
    bool is_head_request_{};
    bool is_grpc_request_{};
    // Tracks if headers other than 100-Continue have been encoded to the codec.
    bool non_100_response_headers_encoded_{};
    // True under the stack of onLocalReply, false otherwise.
    bool under_on_local_reply_{};
    // True when the filter chain iteration was aborted with local reply.
    bool decoder_filter_chain_aborted_{};
    bool encoder_filter_chain_aborted_{};
    bool saw_downstream_reset_{};
    // True when the stream was recreated.
    bool recreated_stream_{};

    // The following 3 members are booleans rather than part of the space-saving bitfield as they
    // are passed as arguments to functions expecting bools. Extend State using the bitfield
    // where possible.
    bool encoder_filters_streaming_{true};
    bool decoder_filters_streaming_{true};
    bool destroyed_{false};

    // Result of filter chain creation.
    CreateChainResult create_chain_result_;

    // Used to track which filter is the latest filter that has received data.
    ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* latest_data_encoding_filter_{};
    ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* latest_data_decoding_filter_{};
  };

  State& state() { return state_; }

private:
  friend class DownstreamFilterManager;
  class FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl : public Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks {
  public:
    FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl(FilterManager& manager)
        : manager_(manager), route_(manager_.streamInfo().route()) {}

    void addStreamDecoderFilter(Http::StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr filter) override {
      manager_.filters_.push_back(filter.get());

      manager_.decoder_filters_.entries_.emplace_back(std::make_unique<ActiveStreamDecoderFilter>(
          manager_, std::move(filter), filter_config_name_));
    }

    void addStreamEncoderFilter(Http::StreamEncoderFilterSharedPtr filter) override {
      manager_.filters_.push_back(filter.get());

      manager_.encoder_filters_.entries_.emplace_back(std::make_unique<ActiveStreamEncoderFilter>(
          manager_, std::move(filter), filter_config_name_));
    }

    void addStreamFilter(Http::StreamFilterSharedPtr filter) override {
      manager_.filters_.push_back(filter.get());

      manager_.decoder_filters_.entries_.emplace_back(
          std::make_unique<ActiveStreamDecoderFilter>(manager_, filter, filter_config_name_));
      manager_.encoder_filters_.entries_.emplace_back(std::make_unique<ActiveStreamEncoderFilter>(
          manager_, std::move(filter), filter_config_name_));
    }

    void addAccessLogHandler(AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtr handler) override {
      manager_.access_log_handlers_.push_back(std::move(handler));
    }

    Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() override { return manager_.dispatcher_; }

    absl::string_view filterConfigName() const override { return filter_config_name_; }

    void setFilterConfigName(absl::string_view name) override { filter_config_name_ = name; }

    OptRef<const Router::Route> route() const override { return makeOptRefFromPtr(route_.get()); }

    absl::optional<bool> filterDisabled(absl::string_view config_name) const override {
      return route_ != nullptr ? route_->filterDisabled(config_name) : absl::nullopt;
    }

    const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() const override { return manager_.streamInfo(); }

    RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() const override {
      return manager_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders();
    }

  private:
    FilterManager& manager_;
    absl::string_view filter_config_name_;
    Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route_;
  };

  // Indicates which filter to start the iteration with.
  enum class FilterIterationStartState { AlwaysStartFromNext, CanStartFromCurrent };

  UpgradeResult createUpgradeFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory,
                                         FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl& callbacks);

  // Returns the encoder filter to start iteration with.
  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator
  commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,
                     FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state);
  // Returns the decoder filter to start iteration with.
  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator
  commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,
                     FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state);
  void addDecodedData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming);
  RequestTrailerMap& addDecodedTrailers();
  MetadataMapVector& addDecodedMetadata();
  // Helper function for the case where we have a header only request, but a filter adds a body
  // to it.
  void maybeContinueDecoding(StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator maybe_continue_data_entry);
  void decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream);
  // Sends data through decoding filter chains. filter_iteration_start_state indicates which
  // filter to start the iteration with.
  void decodeData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream,
                  FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state);
  void decodeTrailers(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestTrailerMap& trailers);
  void decodeMetadata(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, MetadataMap& metadata_map);
  void addEncodedData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming);
  ResponseTrailerMap& addEncodedTrailers();
  void encode1xxHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers);
  // As with most of the encode functions, this runs encodeHeaders on various
  // filters before calling encodeHeadersInternal which does final header munging and passes the
  // headers to the encoder.
  void maybeContinueEncoding(StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator maybe_continue_data_entry);
  void encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers,
                     bool end_stream);
  // Sends data through encoding filter chains. filter_iteration_start_state indicates which
  // filter to start the iteration with, and finally calls encodeDataInternal
  // to update stats, do end stream bookkeeping, and send the data to encoder.
  void encodeData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream,
                  FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state);
  void encodeTrailers(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseTrailerMap& trailers);
  void encodeMetadata(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr);

  // Returns true if new metadata is decoded. Otherwise, returns false.
  bool processNewlyAddedMetadata();

  // Returns true if filter has stopped iteration for all frame types. Otherwise, returns false.
  // filter_streaming is the variable to indicate if stream is streaming, and its value may be
  // changed by the function.
  bool handleDataIfStopAll(ActiveStreamFilterBase& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,
                           bool& filter_streaming);

  MetadataMapVector* getRequestMetadataMapVector() {
    if (request_metadata_map_vector_ == nullptr) {
      request_metadata_map_vector_ = std::make_unique<MetadataMapVector>();
    }
    return request_metadata_map_vector_.get();
  }

  bool stopDecoderFilterChain() { return state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_; }

  bool stopEncoderFilterChain() { return state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_; }

  bool isTerminalDecoderFilter(const ActiveStreamDecoderFilter& filter) const;

  FilterManagerCallbacks& filter_manager_callbacks_;
  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher_;
  // This is unset if there is no downstream connection, e.g. for health check or
  // async requests.
  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection_;
  const uint64_t stream_id_;
  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account_;
  const bool proxy_100_continue_;

  StreamDecoderFilters decoder_filters_;
  StreamEncoderFilters encoder_filters_;
  std::vector<StreamFilterBase*> filters_;
  AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector access_log_handlers_;

  // Stores metadata added in the decoding filter that is being processed. Will be cleared before
  // processing the next filter. The storage is created on demand. We need to store metadata
  // temporarily in the filter in case the filter has stopped all while processing headers.
  std::unique_ptr<MetadataMapVector> request_metadata_map_vector_;
  Buffer::InstancePtr buffered_response_data_;
  Buffer::InstancePtr buffered_request_data_;
  uint64_t buffer_limit_{0};
  uint32_t high_watermark_count_{0};
  std::list<DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks*> watermark_callbacks_;
  Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr upstream_options_ =
      std::make_shared<Network::Socket::Options>();
  std::pair<std::string, bool> upstream_override_host_;

  // TODO(snowp): Once FM has been moved to its own file we'll make these private classes of FM,
  // at which point they no longer need to be friends.
  friend ActiveStreamFilterBase;
  friend ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;
  friend ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;

  /**
   * Flags that keep track of which filter calls are currently in progress.
   */
  // clang-format off
    struct FilterCallState {
      static constexpr uint32_t DecodeHeaders   = 0x01;
      static constexpr uint32_t DecodeData      = 0x02;
      static constexpr uint32_t DecodeMetadata  = 0x04;
      static constexpr uint32_t DecodeTrailers  = 0x08;
      static constexpr uint32_t EncodeHeaders   = 0x10;
      static constexpr uint32_t EncodeData      = 0x20;
      static constexpr uint32_t EncodeMetadata  = 0x40;
      static constexpr uint32_t EncodeTrailers  = 0x80;
      // Encode1xxHeaders is a bit of a special state as 1xx
      // headers may be sent during request processing. This state is only used
      // to verify we do not encode1xx headers more than once per
      // filter.
      static constexpr uint32_t Encode1xxHeaders  = 0x100;
      // Used to indicate that one of the following conditions is true:
      // 1. The filter manager is processing the final [En|De]codeData frame.
      // 2. The filter manager is in [En|De]codeHeaders and is processing a
      //    header-only request or response.
      static constexpr uint32_t EndOfStream = 0x200;

      // Masks for filter call state.
      static constexpr uint32_t IsDecodingMask = DecodeHeaders | DecodeData | DecodeMetadata | DecodeTrailers;
      static constexpr uint32_t IsEncodingMask = EncodeHeaders | Encode1xxHeaders | EncodeData | EncodeMetadata | EncodeTrailers;
    };
  // clang-format on

  State state_;
};

// The DownstreamFilterManager has explicit handling to send local replies.
// The UpstreamFilterManager will not, and will instead defer local reply
// management to the DownstreamFilterManager.
class DownstreamFilterManager : public FilterManager {
public:
  DownstreamFilterManager(FilterManagerCallbacks& filter_manager_callbacks,
                          Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, const Network::Connection& connection,
                          uint64_t stream_id, Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account,
                          bool proxy_100_continue, uint32_t buffer_limit,
                          FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory,
                          const LocalReply::LocalReply& local_reply, Http::Protocol protocol,
                          TimeSource& time_source,
                          StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr parent_filter_state,
                          Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager)
      : FilterManager(filter_manager_callbacks, dispatcher, connection, stream_id, account,
                      proxy_100_continue, buffer_limit),
        stream_info_(protocol, time_source, connection.connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr(),
                     StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::FilterChain,
                     std::move(parent_filter_state)),
        local_reply_(local_reply), filter_chain_factory_(filter_chain_factory),
        downstream_filter_load_shed_point_(overload_manager.getLoadShedPoint(
            Server::LoadShedPointName::get().HttpDownstreamFilterCheck)) {
    ENVOY_LOG_ONCE_IF(
        trace, downstream_filter_load_shed_point_ == nullptr,
        "LoadShedPoint envoy.load_shed_points.http_downstream_filter_check is not found. "
        "Is it configured?");
  }
  ~DownstreamFilterManager() override {
    ASSERT(prepared_local_reply_ == nullptr,
           "Filter Manager destroyed without executing prepared local reply");
  }

  // TODO(snowp): This should probably return a StreamInfo instead of the impl.
  StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl& streamInfo() override { return stream_info_; }
  const StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl& streamInfo() const override { return stream_info_; }

  void setDownstreamRemoteAddress(
      const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& downstream_remote_address) {
    stream_info_.setDownstreamRemoteAddress(downstream_remote_address);
  }

  CreateChainResult createDownstreamFilterChain();

  /**
   * Called before local reply is made by the filter manager.
   * @param data the data associated with the local reply.
   */
  void onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data);

  void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,
                      const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,
                      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,
                      absl::string_view details) override;

  /**
   * Whether downstream has observed end_stream.
   */
  bool decoderObservedEndStream() const override { return state_.observed_decode_end_stream_; }

  /**
   * Return true if the timestamp of the downstream end_stream was recorded.
   * For the HCM to handle the case of internal redirects, timeout error replies
   * and stream resets on premature upstream response.
   */
  bool hasLastDownstreamByteReceived() const {
    return streamInfo().downstreamTiming() &&
           streamInfo().downstreamTiming()->lastDownstreamRxByteReceived().has_value();
  }

  bool shouldLoadShed() override {
    return downstream_filter_load_shed_point_ != nullptr &&
           downstream_filter_load_shed_point_->shouldShedLoad();
  }

private:
  /**
   * Sends a local reply by constructing a response and passing it through all the encoder
   * filters. The resulting response will be passed out via the FilterManagerCallbacks.
   * This will be deprecated in favor of prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain.
   */
  void sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(
      bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,
      const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,
      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details);

  /**
   * Prepares a local reply that will be sent along the encoder filters in
   * executeLocalReplyViaFilterChain.
   */
  void prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(
      bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,
      const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,
      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details);

  /**
   * Executes a prepared local reply along the encoder filters.
   */
  void executeLocalReplyIfPrepared() override;

  /**
   * Sends a local reply by constructing a response and skipping the encoder filters. The
   * resulting response will be passed out via the FilterManagerCallbacks.
   */
  void sendDirectLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,
                            const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,
                            bool is_head_request,
                            const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status);

private:
  OverridableRemoteConnectionInfoSetterStreamInfo stream_info_;
  const LocalReply::LocalReply& local_reply_;
  const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory_;
  Utility::PreparedLocalReplyPtr prepared_local_reply_{nullptr};
  Server::LoadShedPoint* downstream_filter_load_shed_point_{nullptr};
};

arguments.content:
# Envoy HTTP Connection Manager: Architecture Document

## Overview

The HTTP Connection Manager (HCM) is Envoy's core subsystem for processing HTTP traffic. It is implemented as a `Network::ReadFilter` — a plugin in Envoy's L4 filter chain — that takes ownership of a downstream TCP (or QUIC) connection and converts the raw byte stream into a sequence of HTTP request/response lifecycle events. Four primary components collaborate to move a request from arrival on the wire to dispatch upstream and back:

1. **`ConnectionManagerImpl`** — owns the connection and its codec; creates `ActiveStream` objects per request.
2. **`FilterManager` / `DownstreamFilterManager`** — owns the per-stream HTTP filter chain and drives it forward.
3. **Router `Filter`** — the terminal decoder filter; selects a cluster, obtains a connection pool, and forwards the request.
4. **`ClusterManagerImpl`** — manages cluster topology, load balancing, and connection pools.

The source code lives primarily under:

| Component | Source files |
|---|---|
| ConnectionManagerImpl | `source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.{h,cc}` |
| FilterManager | `source/common/http/filter_manager.{h,cc}` |
| Router Filter | `source/common/router/router.{h,cc}`, `source/common/router/upstream_request.{h,cc}` |
| ClusterManager | `source/common/upstream/cluster_manager_impl.{h,cc}` |
| HTTP/1 codec | `source/common/http/http1/` |
| HTTP/2 codec | `source/common/http/http2/` |
| Connection pools | `source/common/http/conn_pool_base.{h,cc}`, `source/common/http/http1/conn_pool.*`, `source/common/http/http2/conn_pool.*`, `source/common/http/conn_pool_grid.*` |
| Outlier detection | `source/common/upstream/outlier_detection_impl.{h,cc}` |
| Health checking | `source/common/upstream/health_checker_impl.{h,cc}` |

---

## 1. ConnectionManagerImpl

### Responsibilities

`ConnectionManagerImpl` (`source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h`) is the network-layer entry point for HTTP traffic. It implements three interfaces simultaneously:

- **`Network::ReadFilter`** — receives raw bytes arriving on the downstream TCP connection.
- **`Http::ServerConnectionCallbacks`** — receives per-stream notifications from the HTTP codec (new stream, GoAway).
- **`Network::ConnectionCallbacks`** — receives connection-level events (close, watermark changes).

It is instantiated once per downstream connection by the HCM extension factory (`source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.cc`) and wired into Envoy's L4 filter chain at connection acceptance time.

### Codec Creation (Lazy Instantiation)

For HTTP/1.x and HTTP/2, `ConnectionManagerImpl` does not create a codec until the first `onData()` call arrives:

```
// source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc, onData()
if (!codec_) {
    createCodec(data);
}
const Status status = codec_->dispatch(data);
```

`createCodec()` delegates to `ConnectionManagerConfig::createCodec()`, which inspects the first bytes of `data` to distinguish HTTP/1 from HTTP/2 (the H2 client preface `PRI * HTTP/2.0\r\n\r\nSM\r\n\r\n`). The resulting `ServerConnection` (H1 or H2 implementation) is stored in `codec_`.

For HTTP/3 (QUIC), the protocol is known at connection time, so the codec is created eagerly in `onNewConnection()` before any `onData()` arrives:

```
// source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc, onNewConnection()
createCodec(dummy);
ASSERT(codec_->protocol() == Protocol::Http3);
return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration; // QUIC bypasses onData entirely
```

This means H3 connections stop L4 filter iteration immediately; QUICHE handles stream demultiplexing internally and delivers decoded events directly to the codec callbacks.

### ActiveStream Creation

When the codec parses a complete set of request headers from the wire, it calls back `ConnectionManagerImpl::newStream()`. This function:

1. Allocates a `Buffer::BufferMemoryAccount` for watermark tracking, wired to the downstream `ResponseEncoder`.
2. Constructs an `ActiveStream` object, giving it a back-reference to the `ConnectionManagerImpl` and a `DownstreamFilterManager`.
3. Prepends the new stream to the `streams_` linked list and returns it as the `RequestDecoder` for the codec to use for subsequent `decodeHeaders` / `decodeData` / `decodeTrailers` calls.

```cpp
// source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc
auto new_stream = std::make_unique<ActiveStream>(
    *this, response_encoder.getStream().bufferLimit(), std::move(downstream_stream_account));
LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(new_stream), streams_);
return **streams_.begin();
```

`ActiveStream` is a heavyweight struct that aggregates the `DownstreamFilterManager`, cached route, request/response header maps, timers, and tracing span for a single HTTP exchange. It implements `RequestDecoder` so the codec can push bytes into it, and `FilterManagerCallbacks` so the filter chain can push encoded responses back toward the codec.

### Connection-Level Concerns

**Drain decisions.** `ConnectionManagerImpl` holds a reference to `Network::DrainDecision`. On each new stream, it checks `maxRequestsPerConnection`; when that limit is reached on H2/H3, it calls `startDrainSequence()` which sends a GOAWAY frame and transitions to `DrainState::Draining`. When the stream count reaches zero, `checkForDeferredClose()` closes the TCP connection.

**Overload management.** Two `Server::LoadShedPoint` handles are acquired at construction from `OverloadManager`:
- `HcmDecodeHeaders` — checked at `decodeHeaders` time; sheds load by sending 503 and stopping iteration.
- `HcmCodecCreation` — checked in `onData()` before codec instantiation; closes the connection before any work is done.

Additionally, two `OverloadActionState` references — `overload_stop_accepting_requests_ref_` and `overload_disable_keepalive_ref_` — are cached at construction and checked in the hot path without requiring a map lookup.

**Watermark-based flow control.** The `ConnectionManagerImpl` implements `onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark()` and `onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark()`, which delegate directly into the codec:

```cpp
void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {
    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();
}
```

The H2 codec propagates these signals to per-stream flow control windows; the H1 codec pauses reads on the connection. Individual `ActiveStream` objects also track watermark state and propagate it to the decoder filter chain via `FilterManager::onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark()`.

**Request deferral across I/O cycles.** The runtime key `http.max_requests_per_io_cycle` limits how many requests can be fully processed in a single read event. Additional requests are deferred to a schedulable callback (`deferred_request_processing_callback_`). This prevents a single connection from monopolizing the event loop under H2 or H3 multiplexing.

**Timers per connection.** `initializeReadFilterCallbacks()` arms:
- `connection_idle_timer_` — fires `onIdleTimeout()` if no streams are active for the configured duration.
- `connection_duration_timer_` — fires `onConnectionDurationTimeout()` after the maximum connection age.

These are _connection-level_ timers that coexist with the _per-stream_ timers managed inside `ActiveStream`.

### Error Handling

Codec errors (protocol violations, buffer flood attacks, inbound frames with empty payloads, overload errors) are caught at the `codec_->dispatch(data)` call site. `handleCodecError()` and `handleCodecOverloadError()` record the appropriate response flag, then call `doConnectionClose()` with `FlushWriteAndDelay`, which:
1. Resets all active streams with `StreamResetReason::ConnectionTermination`.
2. Calls `connection().close()` on the underlying network connection.

---

## 2. FilterManager and the HTTP Filter Chain

### Class Hierarchy

`FilterManager` (`source/common/http/filter_manager.h`) is the abstract base; `DownstreamFilterManager` is the concrete subclass used for downstream (client-to-proxy) streams. A separate `UpstreamFilterManager` is used for the upstream side inside `UpstreamRequest`. `FilterManager` owns two parallel vectors:

```cpp
// source/common/http/filter_manager.h
struct StreamDecoderFilters {
    std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr> entries_;
    // Iterates forward: A -> B -> C
};

struct StreamEncoderFilters {
    std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr> entries_;
    // Iterates in reverse: C -> B -> A
};
```

Each entry wraps a user-provided filter handle (`StreamDecoderFilter`, `StreamEncoderFilter`, or `StreamFilter` for dual-purpose) and tracks per-filter iteration state.

### Filter Chain Construction

`FilterChainFactory::createFilterChain()` is called once per stream during `ActiveStream` initialization, before the first `decodeHeaders` call. The HCM configuration object (`source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.cc`) implements `FilterChainFactory`. It iterates over the ordered list of `HttpFilterFactory` functions configured via `http_filters`, calling:

```cpp
callbacks.addStreamDecoderFilter(filter);
callbacks.addStreamEncoderFilter(filter);
// or, for dual-purpose filters:
callbacks.addStreamFilter(filter);
```

These append `ActiveStreamDecoderFilter` or `ActiveStreamEncoderFilter` wrappers to the respective vectors. The router filter (the terminal filter) is always last in the decoder chain; the `UpstreamCodecFilter` is always last in the upstream filter chain.

### Decoder Filter Iteration (Request Path)

When `ActiveStream::decodeHeaders()` is called by the codec, it validates the header map (if UHV is enabled) and then calls `FilterManager::decodeHeaders()`. This function iterates `decoder_filters_` from beginning to end, calling each filter's `decodeHeaders(headers, end_stream)` method:

```cpp
// source/common/http/filter_manager.cc
for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {
    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeHeaders(headers, end_stream);
    const auto continue_iteration =
        (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);
    if (!continue_iteration && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {
        maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);
        return; // pause here
    }
}
```

A filter controls iteration by its return value:

| Return value | Effect |
|---|---|
| `FilterHeadersStatus::Continue` | Proceed to the next filter immediately. |
| `FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration` | Pause iteration; filter must later call `continueDecoding()`. |
| `FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer` | Pause and buffer all subsequent data until resumed. |
| `FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark` | Pause and apply watermark-based back pressure. |
| `FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream` | Continue but strip the end-stream flag (allows injecting a body). |

`decodeData()` and `decodeTrailers()` follow the same pattern. Each call records a `latest_data_decoding_filter_` pointer so that subsequent data injected by a filter is only forwarded to filters _after_ the injecting filter, preventing double-processing.

### Resuming Iteration

`ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::continueDecoding()` calls `commonContinue()` on the base class. This:
1. Sets `iteration_state_` back to `Continue`.
2. If the filter had stopped with `StopAll`, sets `iterate_from_current_filter_ = true` so iteration restarts from the paused filter rather than the next one.
3. Resumes `do1xxHeaders()`, `doHeaders()`, `doData()`, and `doTrailers()` in order.

### Encoder Filter Iteration (Response Path)

When the router filter receives a response from upstream, it calls back into the `FilterManager` via `FilterManagerCallbacks::encodeHeaders()`. The `FilterManager` iterates `encoder_filters_` in **reverse** order (last-configured to first-configured), ensuring symmetric wrapping: a filter that transforms the request also wraps the response.

```cpp
// source/common/http/filter_manager.h
struct StreamEncoderFilters {
    using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;
    Iterator begin() { return entries_.rbegin(); }
    Iterator end()   { return entries_.rend(); }
};
```

### Local Replies

Any filter may call `sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details)` to terminate the request without forwarding it upstream. This:
1. Sets a `LocalReplyOwnerObject` in filter state identifying the originating filter.
2. Delegates to `FilterManager::sendLocalReply()`, which synthesizes a response header map and runs it through the encoder filter chain.
3. Sets `decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true`, causing all subsequent decoder filter calls to short-circuit.

### Filter Type Taxonomy

| Interface | Direction | When used |
|---|---|---|
| `StreamDecoderFilter` | Request only | Auth filters, body inspection, header manipulation |
| `StreamEncoderFilter` | Response only | Response compression, header stripping |
| `StreamFilter` | Both | External processing (ext_proc), WASM, Lua filters |

All three share `StreamFilterBase::onDestroy()` for cleanup when the stream ends.

### Access Logging

`FilterManager` accumulates access log handlers from two sources: the HCM-level `accessLogs()` list from `ConnectionManagerConfig`, and per-filter handlers added via `FilterManagerCallbacks::accessLogHandlers()`. These are merged in `ActiveStream::accessLogHandlers()` and fired on stream completion.

---

## 3. Router Filter

### Role in the Pipeline

The router filter (`source/common/router/router.h`, class `Filter`) is always the last filter in the decoder chain. It implements `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` and `Upstream::LoadBalancerContextBase`. Unlike most filters that transform traffic and pass it along, the router filter consumes the request and originates a new one upstream via `UpstreamRequest`.

### Route Selection

In `Filter::decodeHeaders()`, after validation of strict check headers, route selection proceeds:

```cpp
// source/common/router/router.cc
route_ = callbacks_->route();
```

`callbacks_->route()` calls into `ActiveStream::route()`, which consults the snapped `RouteConfiguration` (an `ActiveStream`-local snapshot of the RDS configuration taken when the stream begins). The route configuration (`source/common/router/config_impl.cc`) evaluates each `VirtualHost` and its `Route` entries in configuration order, matching on `:authority`, path prefix/regex/exact, headers, and query parameters. The first match wins.

If no route matches, the router sends a 404 via `sendLocalReply()` and returns `StopIteration`.

If the matched route has a `direct_response`, the router synthesizes the configured response locally and never contacts upstream.

If the matched route has a `route_entry`, the router extracts `route_entry_->clusterName()` and looks up the cluster:

```cpp
Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster* cluster =
    config_->cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(route_entry_->clusterName());
```

This lookup is thread-local and lock-free (see §4). If the cluster does not exist, a 503 with `NoClusterFound` response flag is returned.

### Host Selection and Connection Pool Acquisition

After cluster lookup, the router calls `cluster->chooseHost(this)` where `this` implements `LoadBalancerContext`. This interface provides:
- `computeHashKey()` — for consistent-hash load balancers, derived from header-based hash policy configured on the route or cluster.
- `metadataMatchCriteria()` — for metadata-based subset routing.
- `downstreamConnection()` — allows the LB to read downstream SSL/connection attributes.

Host selection may be synchronous (common case) or asynchronous (e.g., when waiting for an on-demand cluster). In the synchronous path, `continueDecodeHeaders()` is called immediately. In the asynchronous path, the filter returns `StopAllIterationAndWatermark` and stores a callback to resume when the host is ready.

Once a host is selected, `createConnPool()` obtains a `GenericConnPool` from the cluster's factory:

```cpp
// source/common/router/router.cc
std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> generic_conn_pool = createConnPool(*cluster, selected_host);
```

The `GenericConnPool` wraps an HTTP or TCP connection pool selected based on the route's `upstream_protocol` (HTTP, TCP for CONNECT tunneling, or UDP for CONNECT-UDP).

### UpstreamRequest Lifecycle

`UpstreamRequest` (`source/common/router/upstream_request.h`) encapsulates a single attempt to forward the request to a specific upstream host. The router may hold multiple `UpstreamRequest` objects simultaneously (one primary + hedged retries).

```cpp
UpstreamRequestPtr upstream_request = std::make_unique<UpstreamRequest>(
    *this, std::move(generic_conn_pool), can_send_early_data,
    can_use_http3, enable_half_close);
LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(upstream_request), upstream_requests_);
upstream_requests_.front()->acceptHeadersFromRouter(end_stream);
```

Internally, `UpstreamRequest` owns an `UpstreamFilterManager` (a second, upstream-side filter chain). Headers and body flow through this chain until they reach the terminal `UpstreamCodecFilter`, which writes them to the upstream HTTP codec. If the upstream connection is not yet ready, `UpstreamCodecFilter` returns `StopAllIteration`, causing the `UpstreamFilterManager` to buffer data and apply watermarks back toward the router.

When the upstream connection pool signals success via `GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks::onPoolReady()`, the `UpstreamCodecFilter` flushes buffered data and begins streaming.

Response data arriving from upstream travels the reverse path: the upstream codec delivers frames to `UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders()` / `decodeData()` / `decodeTrailers()`, which feed back through the `UpstreamFilterManager` and then call `RouterFilterInterface` callbacks on the `Filter`, which in turn calls `FilterManagerCallbacks::encodeHeaders()` to begin the downstream encoder filter chain.

### Retry Logic

`retry_state_` is created in `continueDecodeHeaders()` from the route's `RetryPolicy`:

```cpp
retry_state_ = createRetryState(*getEffectiveRetryPolicy(), headers, *cluster_,
                                 request_vcluster_, route_stats_context_,
                                 config_->factory_context_, callbacks_->dispatcher(),
                                 route_entry_->priority());
```

`RetryStateImpl` (`source/common/router/retry_state_impl.cc`) tracks the number of attempts, retry-on conditions (5xx, reset, connect-failure, retriable status codes), and the current backoff interval. When an `UpstreamRequest` fails (connection error, 5xx response, per-try timeout), the router calls `retry_state_->shouldRetry()`. If retrying, a new `UpstreamRequest` is constructed and prepended to `upstream_requests_`, and the failed one is reset and removed.

### Timeout Handling

`FilterUtility::finalTimeout()` computes two timeouts:

- **Global timeout** — enforced by a timer on `ActiveStream` (`max_stream_duration_timer_`). When it fires, the router sends a 504 locally.
- **Per-try timeout** — set up via `UpstreamRequest::setupPerTryTimeout()`. When it fires, the current attempt is reset and retried (if `hedge_on_per_try_timeout` is configured, a new attempt is started _before_ the old one is cancelled, enabling hedging).

### Shadow Routing

In `continueDecodeHeaders()`, after the primary `UpstreamRequest` is created, the router evaluates shadow policies from the route or cluster configuration. For each active shadow:

```cpp
Http::AsyncClient::OngoingRequest* shadow_stream =
    config_->shadowWriter().streamingShadow(cluster_name, shadow_headers, options);
```

`ShadowWriterImpl` (`source/common/router/shadow_writer_impl.cc`) sends the mirrored request via the HTTP async client. Shadow responses are discarded. Shadow streams are tracked in `shadow_streams_` and cancelled on stream destruction.

### Error Handling

| Failure | Router action |
|---|---|
| No route | `sendLocalReply(404, RouteNotFound)` |
| Cluster not found | `sendLocalReply(503, ClusterNotFound)` |
| No healthy upstream | `sendLocalReply(503, NoHealthyUpstream)` |
| Maintenance mode | `sendLocalReply(503, MaintenanceMode)` |
| Connection pool failure | Reset `UpstreamRequest`; check `retry_state_` |
| Upstream 5xx / reset | Consult `retry_state_`; retry or propagate 5xx downstream |
| Global timeout | `sendLocalReply(504, ResponseTimeout)` |
| Per-try timeout (hedging off) | Reset attempt; retry if budget allows |

---

## 4. Cluster Manager and Upstream Connectivity

### ClusterManagerImpl Architecture

`ClusterManagerImpl` (`source/common/upstream/cluster_manager_impl.h`) is a singleton that maintains all cluster state in a thread-safe manner. Because Envoy uses a single-threaded event loop per worker thread, the cluster manager uses a thread-local storage pattern:

- **Main thread** holds authoritative `ClusterData` objects (the `active_clusters_` and `warming_clusters_` maps).
- **Each worker thread** holds a `ThreadLocalClusterManagerImpl` with its own `thread_local_clusters_` map, each containing per-host connection pools.

Cluster updates (from xDS/CDS) are applied on the main thread and then posted to all worker threads via `tls_.runOnAllThreads()`. This means each worker thread sees a consistent snapshot of cluster state without locks.

The `getThreadLocalCluster(name)` method (called by the router on the hot path) performs a simple hash map lookup against `thread_local_clusters_` — no locking, no cross-thread communication.

### Load Balancing

Each `ThreadLocalClusterManagerImpl::ClusterEntry` owns a `LoadBalancer` instance specific to that worker thread. When the router calls `cluster->chooseHost(context)`, it delegates to `lb_->chooseHost(context)`. Envoy ships several built-in load balancer implementations:

- **Round robin** (`source/common/upstream/round_robin_lb.cc`) — cycles through the healthy host set.
- **Least request** (`source/common/upstream/least_request_lb.cc`) — picks the host with fewest active requests using O(2) power-of-two-choices sampling.
- **Random** (`source/common/upstream/random_lb.cc`) — uniform random selection.
- **Ring hash / Maglev** (`source/common/upstream/ring_hash_lb.cc`, `maglev_lb.cc`) — consistent hashing; uses `LoadBalancerContext::computeHashKey()`.
- **Subset LB** (`source/common/upstream/subset_lb.cc`) — wraps another LB; selects hosts matching `MetadataMatchCriteria`.

All built-in LBs operate on the **priority set** — an ordered collection of `HostSet` objects, one per priority level. Hosts that fail health checks or outlier detection are removed from the healthy set and are not selected.

### Connection Pool Mechanics

`ClusterEntry::httpConnPool()` (in `cluster_manager_impl.cc`) returns an `HttpPoolData` wrapping an `Http::ConnectionPool::Instance`:

```cpp
absl::optional<HttpPoolData>
ClusterEntry::httpConnPool(HostConstSharedPtr host, ResourcePriority priority,
                           absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol,
                           LoadBalancerContext* context) {
    auto pool = httpConnPoolImpl(host, priority, protocol, context);
    ...
    HttpPoolData data([this, priority, protocol, context]() {
        maybePreconnect(...); // preconnect on each new stream
    }, pool);
    return data;
}
```

Each pool is keyed by `(host, priority, protocol)`. Pools are created lazily on first use and are stored in `PriorityConnPoolMap<Http::ConnectionPool::Instance>`.

The concrete pool classes are:
- `Http1ConnPoolImpl` (`source/common/http/http1/conn_pool.cc`) — one request per TCP connection; connections are not reused until the response is complete.
- `Http2ConnPoolImpl` (`source/common/http/http2/conn_pool.cc`) — multiplexes many streams over one TCP connection; governed by `max_concurrent_streams`.
- `ConnPoolGrid` (`source/common/http/conn_pool_grid.cc`) — tries HTTP/3 first (if QUIC is available), with automatic fallback to HTTP/2 or HTTP/1. Uses `HttpServerPropertiesCache` to remember per-origin protocol capabilities.

The shared base `HttpConnPoolImplBase` (`source/common/http/conn_pool_base.h`) implements stream queuing via `HttpPendingStream` objects. When `newStream()` is called:
1. If a ready connection exists with capacity, the stream is attached immediately via `onPoolReady()`.
2. Otherwise, a `HttpPendingStream` is enqueued. When a connection becomes available (either an existing one frees capacity, or a new one finishes the TLS handshake), the pending stream is dequeued and attached.

**Preconnect.** After each new stream is established, `maybePreconnect()` is called speculatively to open an additional connection if the predicted load warrants it. The decision is based on `peekahead_ratio` and current `pending_streams_ / connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_` ratios, mirroring the logic in `ConnPoolImplBase::shouldConnect()`.

### Health Checking

Health checkers (`source/common/upstream/health_checker_impl.cc`) run periodic synthetic requests (HTTP GET to the health check path, TCP connect, gRPC health check). Results are tracked as `HealthFlag` bits on each `Host`:

- `FAILED_ACTIVE_HC` — active health check has determined the host is unhealthy.
- `FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK` — outlier detection has ejected the host.
- `FAILED_EDS_HEALTH` — xDS EDS marked the host unhealthy.

On a health check failure, the health checker calls `postThreadLocalHealthFailure(host)`, which posts to all worker threads to update the host's health flag and re-calculate the healthy host set. Because `LoadBalancer::chooseHost()` only draws from the healthy set, newly unhealthy hosts are excluded from selection within a single event loop cycle.

### Outlier Detection

`DetectorImpl` (`source/common/upstream/outlier_detection_impl.cc`) monitors per-host outcome data (5xx rate, consecutive 5xx, response latency via EWMA, success rate across the cluster). When a host's metrics cross a configured threshold, the detector:

1. Sets `Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK`.
2. Calls the registered `addChangedStateCb` on `ClusterManagerImpl`, which posts `postThreadLocalHealthFailure(host)` to all workers.
3. Schedules an ejection timer; after the ejection duration, the host is returned to the healthy set.

The router's `UpstreamRequest` reports upstream outcomes back to the outlier detector on stream completion, closing the feedback loop:

```
UpstreamRequest completion
  -> Router::Filter::onUpstreamComplete()
  -> host->outlierDetector().putHttpResponseCode(status_code)
  -> DetectorImpl checks thresholds
  -> eject host if threshold exceeded
  -> postThreadLocalHealthFailure -> LB removes host from healthy set
```

---

## 5. End-to-End Data Flow

### Request Path (Downstream to Upstream)

```
1. TCP bytes arrive on downstream connection
   -> ConnectionManagerImpl::onData()
   -> codec_->dispatch(data)            [H1/H2 codec parses bytes]

2. Codec sees complete request headers
   -> ConnectionManagerImpl::newStream()  [creates ActiveStream]
   -> ActiveStream::decodeHeaders()       [validates, traces, initializes timers]
   -> FilterManager::decodeHeaders()      [iterates decoder filter chain A->B->...->Router]

3. Each decoder filter processes headers
   -> returns Continue / StopIteration / StopAllIterationAndBuffer

4. Router filter (terminal decoder filter) receives headers
   -> route_ = callbacks_->route()                 [RouteConfiguration match]
   -> cluster = cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(name)    [TLS lookup, no lock]
   -> host = cluster->chooseHost(this)             [LB selects endpoint]
   -> generic_conn_pool = createConnPool(cluster, host)
   -> UpstreamRequest created; acceptHeadersFromRouter() called

5. UpstreamRequest feeds UpstreamFilterManager
   -> Traverses upstream filter chain -> UpstreamCodecFilter
   -> If connection ready: codec writes headers to upstream TCP connection
   -> If not ready: UpstreamCodecFilter buffers; watermark back-pressure propagates

6. Body and trailers follow same path via decodeData() / decodeTrailers()
```

### Response Path (Upstream to Downstream)

```
1. Upstream bytes arrive and are parsed by upstream codec
   -> UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders() / decodeData() / decodeTrailers()

2. Response flows up through UpstreamFilterManager (upstream encoder direction)
   -> UpstreamCodecFilter -> ... -> UpstreamRequest callbacks

3. Router::Filter receives response
   -> Checks retry_state_: retry or propagate?
   -> On propagate: FilterManagerCallbacks::encodeHeaders() called

4. FilterManager::encodeHeaders() iterates encoder_filters_ in REVERSE order
   -> ...filter B -> filter A -> terminal (writes to ResponseEncoder)

5. ActiveStream::encodeHeaders() writes to codec ResponseEncoder
   -> H1/H2/H3 codec serializes headers to downstream TCP write buffer

6. Connection-level watermarks propagate if write buffer fills:
   -> onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() -> codec -> per-stream flow control
   -> Router suspends upstream reads; upstream TCP window closes
```

### Stream Teardown

After the response is fully encoded:
1. `ActiveStream::endStream()` is called, setting `codec_saw_local_complete_ = true`.
2. `ConnectionManagerImpl::doEndStream()` checks whether the stream can be destroyed (`canDestroyStream()` — true when `codec_encode_complete_` or `on_reset_stream_called_` are set).
3. `doDeferredStreamDestroy()` disarms all per-stream timers, calls `filter_manager_.onStreamComplete()` (which fires access logs), and schedules the `ActiveStream` for deferred deletion via `dispatcher_->deferredDelete()`.
4. On H1, if the stream indicated drain-on-completion, `checkForDeferredClose()` transitions to `DrainState::Closing` and closes the connection.

---

## 6. Extension Points

The HCM is designed as an extension platform. The primary extension mechanisms are:

### HTTP Filters

The most important extension point. Any code implementing `StreamDecoderFilter`, `StreamEncoderFilter`, or `StreamFilter` and registered via the `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` extension registry can be inserted at any position in the `http_filters` list (except after the router, which must be terminal).

Notable built-in extensions under `source/extensions/filters/http/`:
- `ext_proc` — sends headers/body to an external gRPC server for transformation.
- `wasm` — runs WebAssembly filters.
- `lua` — inline Lua scripts.
- `jwt_authn`, `oauth2`, `ext_authz` — authentication and authorization.
- `grpc_json_transcoder` — translates REST to gRPC.
- `buffer`, `local_ratelimit`, `ratelimit` — traffic shaping.
- `compression` / `decompression` — gzip/brotli/zstd codecs.

**Per-route filter configuration.** Each filter may expose `RouteSpecificFilterConfig` that overrides its behavior per virtual host or route. Filters access this via `StreamFilterCallbacks::mostSpecificPerFilterConfig()`.

### Cluster Extensions

Custom cluster types implement `ClusterFactory` and are registered under `envoy.cluster`. They control host discovery (e.g., EDS, STRICT_DNS, LOGICAL_DNS, custom Redis or aggregate clusters).

### Load Balancer Extensions

Custom load balancers implement `TypedLoadBalancerFactory`. The per-worker `LoadBalancer` is created by `TypedLoadBalancerFactory::create()`. Registered under `envoy.load_balancing_policies`.

### Connection Pool / Upstream Transport Extensions

`GenericConnPoolFactory` (registered under `envoy.upstreams`) controls how upstream connections are established. This is the hook for CONNECT tunneling, UDP proxying, or custom upstream protocols.

### Access Loggers

Implement `AccessLog::Instance` and register under `envoy.access_loggers`. Access loggers are called by `ActiveStream::log()` at stream start (periodically if configured), on stream completion, and optionally on upstream reset. Built-in loggers write to files, gRPC streams (ALS), or the OpenTelemetry logging API.

### Health Check Extensions

Custom health checkers implement `HealthCheckerFactory` (`envoy.health_checkers`). The `ClusterManagerImpl` wires the health checker to the cluster via `Cluster::healthChecker()`.

### Request ID Extensions

Implement `RequestIDExtension` to control how `x-request-id` is generated and propagated. The HCM calls `requestIDExtension()->setInRequest()` and `setInResponse()` at stream start and end.

---

## 7. Error Handling Summary

| Stage | Error condition | Handling |
|---|---|---|
| Codec (H1/H2 parse) | Protocol violation, buffer flood | `handleCodecError()` → close connection with `FlushWriteAndDelay` |
| Codec (overload) | `hcm_ondata_creating_codec_` sheds load | `handleCodecOverloadError()` → close connection |
| Header validation (UHV) | Invalid request headers | `ActiveStream::validateHeaders()` sends 400; returns false to abort filter chain |
| Decoder filter | Filter calls `sendLocalReply()` | Sets `decoder_filter_chain_aborted_`; runs encoder chain with synthetic response |
| Decoder filter | Filter throws exception | Caught by Envoy's event loop; connection is closed |
| Route selection | No matching route | Router sends 404 with `RouteNotFound` flag |
| Cluster lookup | Cluster not found or maintenance | Router sends 503 with `NoClusterFound` / `UpstreamOverflow` |
| Host selection | No healthy hosts | `sendNoHealthyUpstreamResponse()` → 503 |
| Connection pool | Connection failure | `onPoolFailure()` → `retry_state_->shouldRetry()` or 503 |
| Upstream 5xx/reset | Bad upstream response | Router checks `retry_state_`; retries or sends upstream status downstream |
| Per-try timeout | Attempt exceeds per-try deadline | `UpstreamRequest` reset; retry or 504 |
| Global timeout | Stream exceeds max duration | `onStreamMaxDurationReached()` → 504 |
| Outlier detection | Host exceeds error thresholds | Host ejected from healthy set; future `chooseHost()` calls avoid it |
| Downstream disconnect | Remote or local close during stream | `resetAllStreams()` with `ConnectionTermination`; access logs still fire |
| Zombie streams | Codec encode complete but response encoder still exists | Stream tracked as zombie; `doEndStream()` deferred until codec signals completion |
